BMAD Method v6.0.0-alpha.23 Upgrade Experience: A Fresh Install Journey

Jan 15, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Upgrading BMAD Method from alpha.19 to alpha.23 turned into an unexpected fresh install journey. Here’s what happened, what changed, and why it might be the right time for you to upgrade too.

The Version Gap Problem

Section titled “The Version Gap Problem”

When I ran bunx bmad-method@alpha install, I was greeted with a version warning:

The BMAD team has implemented a sensible stability policy—only supporting updates from the previous 2 alpha versions. This prevents accumulation of technical debt and configuration drift.

The Fresh Install Decision

Section titled “The Fresh Install Decision”

Given that I was 4 versions behind, the recommended path was clear:

  1. Delete the _bmad/ folder in my project
  2. Run a fresh installation

Benefits of a fresh install:

  • Cleaner configuration without legacy artifacts
  • All new features properly configured
  • Fewer potential conflicts

The alternative (canceling and trying to upgrade anyway) would have risked subtle incompatibilities.

The Installation Process

Section titled “The Installation Process”

With BMAD uninstalled, I ran the installer again:

The new alpha.23 installer has several welcome improvements:

1. Comprehensive Module Selection

Section titled “1. Comprehensive Module Selection”

The installer now offers:

  • BMB: BMad Builder (Agent, Workflow, and Module Builder)
  • BMGD: BMad Game Development
  • BMM: BMad Method Agile-AI-Driven-Development
  • CIS: Creative Innovation Suite

2. Enhanced Tool Configuration

Section titled “2. Enhanced Tool Configuration”

I was prompted to configure tools for:

  • Claude Code ⭐
  • Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot
  • Google Antigravity, OpenCode, Windsurf
  • And 10+ others (Auggie CLI, Cline, Crush, Gemini CLI, etc.)

3. TTS Support Option

Section titled “3. TTS Support Option”

New in alpha.23: Claude Code TTS (Text-to-Speech) support configuration prompt.

What’s New in Alpha.23

Section titled “What’s New in Alpha.23”

New Documentation Site

Section titled “New Documentation Site”

The installer highlights a brand new documentation resource:

📚 New Docs Site: http://docs.bmad-method.org/

  • Tons of guides and articles coming soon!
  • Everything is free. No paywalls. No gated content.
  • Knowledge should be shared, not sold.

This is a significant community investment—free, comprehensive documentation accessible to everyone.

Beta Timeline

Section titled “Beta Timeline”

According to the installer output:

Stable Beta coming soon - please read the full README.md and linked documentation to get started!

The team is pushing toward beta, with the fresh install requirement expected to be lifted around January 18th.

Community Resources

Section titled “Community Resources”
  • GitHub: https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD/
  • YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@BMadCode (Master Class & Podcast launching February 1st!)
  • Changelog: https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

Configuration Improvements

Section titled “Configuration Improvements”

The fresh install gave me cleaner configuration:

The installer created:

  • 67 workflows
  • 25 agents
  • 4 tasks
  • 96 files

All properly configured for my chosen tools (claude-code, codex, cursor, github-copilot, antigravity, opencode, windsurf, rovo-dev, auggie, cline, crush, gemini, iflow, kilo, kiro-cli, qwen, roo, trae).

Lessons Learned

Section titled “Lessons Learned”

1. Don’t Fall Too Far Behind

Section titled “1. Don’t Fall Too Far Behind”

The alpha.19 → alpha.23 gap taught me to stay within 2 versions. This is good practice for any alpha software.

2. Fresh Installs Are Painless

Section titled “2. Fresh Installs Are Painless”

I was worried about losing configuration, but the fresh install was:

  • Fast (~2 minutes)
  • Interactive and clear
  • Resulted in cleaner setup

3. The Community is Growing

Section titled “3. The Community is Growing”

Between the new docs site, upcoming YouTube content, and GitHub activity, BMAD is rapidly evolving. Staying current means access to better tools and patterns.

Recommendation

Section titled “Recommendation”

If you’re on alpha.21 or earlier, consider upgrading to alpha.23 now. The fresh install requirement is a small inconvenience for a significantly improved setup.

For teams on alpha.19 or earlier: plan your upgrade before the beta release to avoid more complex migrations later.

Next Steps

Section titled “Next Steps”

After upgrading, I recommend:

  1. Read the new documentation at docs.bmad-method.org
  2. Explore the 67 newly installed workflows
  3. Try the Quick Dev workflow: /bmad:bmm:workflows:quick-dev
  4. Subscribe to the YouTube channel for the February 1st launch

Resources:

  • BMAD Method Documentation
  • GitHub Repository
  • YouTube Channel
  • Changelog
Tags:
  • BMAD
  • AI Workflow
  • Upgrade Guide
  • Installation
  • Vibe Coding

BMAD Method Getting Started: A Practical Guide to Quick-Dev Workflows

Jan 14, 2026
Vibe Sparking

BMAD Method (Breakthrough Method of Agile AI‑Driven Development) is an AI-driven development framework that turns “random prompt hacking” into a repeatable delivery system.

It does that by packaging:

  • Specialized agents (PM/Architect/Dev/QA/Docs…)
  • Guided workflows (discovery → planning → execution → verification)
  • Living artifacts (briefs, PRDs, stories, checklists)

…so your project context and quality gates live in the repo, not in someone’s chat history.

1) Features & highlights (what you get)

Section titled “1) Features & highlights (what you get)”

Role-based help instead of ad-hoc prompting

Section titled “Role-based help instead of ad-hoc prompting”

BMAD provides role personas so you can reliably ask for:

  • a product-level framing (PM)
  • architecture tradeoffs and decisions (Architect)
  • implementation guidance and code changes (Dev)
  • verification and test strategy (QA/Test Architect)
  • documentation and release notes (Tech Writer)

Scale-adaptive planning

Section titled “Scale-adaptive planning”

BMAD is designed to “right-size” the process:

  • Quick Dev / Quick Flow: bug fixes, small features, quick iterations
  • Standard track: medium features/products, PRD → architecture → stories
  • Enterprise track: governance-heavy environments, stronger gates and traceability

A closed loop from idea to verification

Section titled “A closed loop from idea to verification”

Instead of generating one-off answers, BMAD encourages a workflow that produces durable artifacts and then uses them to implement and verify.

2) Prerequisites, installation & configuration

Section titled “2) Prerequisites, installation & configuration”

What you’ll need

Section titled “What you’ll need”

BMAD works with any AI coding assistant that supports custom system prompts / project context. Common choices include:

  • Claude Code (CLI)
  • Cursor
  • Windsurf
  • Roo Code (VS Code)
  • GitHub Copilot (VS Code)

Installation (typical)

Section titled “Installation (typical)”

From your project root (Node.js 20+ recommended), install BMAD into the project (not your home dir) so the config is versionable:

After installation, your repo will contain BMAD modules (agents/workflows/templates). From there you can run workflows from your IDE/assistant.

3) Quick Dev workflow (the “fast lane”)

Section titled “3) Quick Dev workflow (the “fast lane”)”

If your BMAD setup exposes slash commands, you can start here:

If you just want to ship something quickly, the mindset is:

  1. Align on the goal and constraints
  2. Produce a minimal plan / acceptance criteria
  3. Implement with tight verification loops

A practical “Quick Dev” run looks like:

Step A — Define the outcome

Section titled “Step A — Define the outcome”

Write down:

  • What “done” means (acceptance criteria)
  • Guardrails (what not to change)
  • How you’ll verify (build/tests/manual steps)

Step B — Use the workflow to generate lightweight artifacts

Section titled “Step B — Use the workflow to generate lightweight artifacts”

Use the Quick Dev workflow to produce a small set of artifacts that are actually useful during implementation:

  • a short task breakdown
  • an implementation checklist
  • a verification checklist

Step C — Execute and verify

Section titled “Step C — Execute and verify”

Have the Dev implement changes and the QA/Test Architect verify with:

  • targeted tests
  • build checks
  • smoke tests

The biggest win is that these outputs are reusable: they become a baseline for the next iteration.

4) Recommended scenarios & daily workflow examples

Section titled “4) Recommended scenarios & daily workflow examples”

Scenario 1 — Fix a bug without losing the plot

Section titled “Scenario 1 — Fix a bug without losing the plot”

Use Quick Dev to:

  • reproduce the bug
  • add a minimal regression test
  • fix the root cause
  • verify the build

Scenario 2 — Add a small feature with predictable scope

Section titled “Scenario 2 — Add a small feature with predictable scope”

Use Quick Dev to:

  • clarify edge cases
  • define acceptance criteria
  • implement incrementally
  • validate UX/behavior quickly

Scenario 3 — Personal knowledge work (docs, blog posts, learning)

Section titled “Scenario 3 — Personal knowledge work (docs, blog posts, learning)”

BMAD isn’t only for code. A simple pattern:

  • Tech Writer drafts an outline + key sections
  • Dev/Architect validate technical correctness
  • QA proofreads for completeness and broken steps

This is especially effective for “tutorial + troubleshooting + references” style posts.

5) Precautions, FAQ & troubleshooting

Section titled “5) Precautions, FAQ & troubleshooting”

“The agent output is too verbose / too shallow”

Section titled ““The agent output is too verbose / too shallow””
  • Ensure your experience level is configured appropriately (expert vs beginner).
  • Use acceptance criteria to constrain the output.
  • Prefer artifacts (brief/spec/story) over free-form chat.

“Context is too large / token limits”

Section titled ““Context is too large / token limits””

BMAD’s document sharding and story-driven approach helps. Keep artifacts scoped and feed only what’s needed to the next role.

“My team doesn’t want heavy ceremony”

Section titled ““My team doesn’t want heavy ceremony””

Start with Quick Dev. If it sticks, gradually adopt more planning where it pays off.

6) Collaboration & comparisons

Section titled “6) Collaboration & comparisons”

BMAD is in the same family of ideas as other “spec-first / structured prompting” approaches (e.g., OpenSpec, Spec Kit), but BMAD’s distinguishing traits are:

  • explicit role personas
  • explicit workflow definitions
  • strong focus on artifact flow and repeatability

7) References

Section titled “7) References”
  • Official docs: https://docs.bmad-method.org/
  • BMAD Method site: https://bmad-method.org
  • GitHub: https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD
Tags:
  • BMAD
  • AI Workflow
  • AI Agents
  • Agile
  • Vibe Coding

HeartPetBond App Launch: GLM 4.7 AI Autonomous Development Passes App Store Review in 2 Days

Jan 12, 2026
Vibe Sparking

First of its kind? This may be the first App developed autonomously by domestic GLM 4.7 LLM to pass App Store review and launch.

Review Passed: 2 Days, First Attempt

Section titled “Review Passed: 2 Days, First Attempt”

Submitted on January 10th, approved on January 12th — taking just over 2 days.

If not submitted on a weekend (January 10th was Saturday), the review time might have been even shorter. Compared to the previous MoneyMind App’s first launch which experienced 14 days, 4 submissions, and 3 rejections, HeartPetBond’s review process was smooth sailing.

Why Did It Pass on First Try?

Section titled “Why Did It Pass on First Try?”

Experience Reuse: Learning from MoneyMind’s Pitfalls

Section titled “Experience Reuse: Learning from MoneyMind’s Pitfalls”

Since I had successfully launched MoneyMind App before, I had encountered many pitfalls. This time, I proactively avoided these issues:

  • Screen Compatibility: MoneyMind was rejected for text overlap issues. This time, AI-autonomous testing prioritized verification across different screen sizes
  • Account Deletion: App Store requires account deletion functionality. This time, it was built-in from the start
  • Feature Completeness: Ensured all promoted features work properly, with no “coming soon” placeholders

Tech Stack: GLM 4.7’s First App Store Debut

Section titled “Tech Stack: GLM 4.7’s First App Store Debut”

HeartPetBond was developed autonomously using domestic Zhipu AI’s GLM 4.7 Large Language Model:

ComponentDescription
Claude CodeBest engineered AI Agent system
GLM 4.7Domestic excellence, extremely cost-effective (200K context, 64K output)
BMAD V6Most powerful AI-driven agile development process
Flutter MCPBest cross-platform development MCP support

Compared to using Opus 4.5 for MoneyMind development, GLM 4.7 costs only about 1/7, but under the BMAD agile process, it’s fully capable of handling App development tasks.

Project Review: From Idea to Launch

Section titled “Project Review: From Idea to Launch”

Timeline

Section titled “Timeline”
  • December 29, 2024: Conceived the HeartPetBond idea (adopted a British Shorthair blue cat)
  • January 10, 2026: Submitted to App Store for review
  • January 12, 2026: Review approved, ready for launch

Development Results

Section titled “Development Results”
MetricValue
Dart Files62 files
Total Lines of Code15,190 lines
AI Generated Code11,131 lines
Main Pages~10 pages

GLM 4.7: Domestic Model’s App Store Debut?

Section titled “GLM 4.7: Domestic Model’s App Store Debut?”

Could this be the first App developed using GLM 4.7 model to successfully pass App Store review and launch globally?

If so, this would be a milestone for domestic large models in AI autonomous development:

  1. Technical Feasibility Verified: GLM 4.7 is fully capable of handling complex App development tasks
  2. Significant Cost Reduction: Development cost is only 1/7 compared to Opus 4.5
  3. Significantly Improved Efficiency: From idea to App Store launch in just 14 days
  4. Quality Assured: Passing review on first attempt demonstrates code quality and feature completeness meet standards

Core Features: 1-Second Recording, Frictionless Tracking

Section titled “Core Features: 1-Second Recording, Frictionless Tracking”

HeartPetBond’s core philosophy is “1-Second Recording, Frictionless Tracking”:

  • Feeding Records: One-tap recording of each feeding
  • Water Changes: Track drinking water replacement
  • Play Time: Record interaction sessions
  • Litter Cleaning: Cleaning frequency reminders
  • Family Collaboration: Whole family participates in pet care together

Key Findings

Section titled “Key Findings”
  1. Domestic Models Work: While GLM 4.7 is slightly behind Opus 4.5, it’s fully capable of App development tasks under the BMAD agile process
  2. Experience is Reusable: Learning from MoneyMind’s review experience made the second App Store submission much smoother
  3. AI Autonomous Development is Mature: From idea to App Store launch, minimal human involvement required
  4. Vibe Coding Era Has Arrived: With good ideas and mature AI toolchains, anyone can quickly turn ideas into reality

Related Reading

Section titled “Related Reading”
  • HeartPetBond App: From Adopting a Cat to AI Autonomous Development App Store Submission
  • MoneyMind App Launch: AI Autonomous Development Passes Apple App Store Review
  • Practical Review: Building HeartPetBond App with Claude Code + BMAD + GLM-4.7

Life is compounding. Build your pet emotional bond with AI!

Tags:
  • HeartPetBond
  • GLM-4.7
  • Claude Code
  • BMAD
  • App Store
  • AI Autonomous Development
  • Vibe Coding

Weekly Review: AI Autonomous Development - Week 2 - From App Store Launch to Batch Content Production

Jan 12, 2026
Vibe Sparking

Core Conclusion: AI autonomous software app development has been validated — 1 week for PoC, 2 weeks to submit to App Store. Humans have no advantage in software development execution.

1. App Store Submission and Launch

Section titled “1. App Store Submission and Launch”

This week’s biggest milestone is successfully validating that Vibe Coding AI-Inspired Programming has achieved a complete closed loop of low-cost, rapid software app development.

MoneyMind App: Published on App Store

Section titled “MoneyMind App: Published on App Store”

The MoneyMind app has finally passed App Store review and is now globally available.

Key Data:

  • Review Cycle: 14 days, 4 submissions, 3 rejections
  • Code Scale: 89,933 lines of code, 133 files, ~40,000 lines of AI-generated code
  • Human Involvement: Less than 4 hours interacting with AI, 8 hours handling App Store submission, 8 hours writing articles
  • AI Autonomous Running: 100+ hours

HeartPetBond App: Submitted for Review

Section titled “HeartPetBond App: Submitted for Review”

Building on MoneyMind’s success, this experiment used the domestic GLM 4.7 model as a cost-effective alternative.

Key Data:

  • Development Cycle: Dec 29 idea → Jan 10 submitted to App Store
  • Code Scale: 62 Dart files, 15,190 lines of code
  • Cost Reduction: GLM 4.7 costs about 1/7 of Opus 4.5
  • Tech Stack: Claude Code + GLM 4.7 + BMAD V6 + Flutter MCP

Validation Conclusion

Section titled “Validation Conclusion”

1 week for AI autonomous PoC and MVP, from idea to App Store submission. Barring issues, 2 weeks to complete App launch.

2. Batch Publishing WeChat Articles

Section titled “2. Batch Publishing WeChat Articles”

Starting January 4, using Claude Code Skills + GLM 4.7 workflow to automatically collect and write 50+ WeChat articles, now published to WeChat draft box. Each article is manually reviewed before publishing.

Workflow Maturity

Section titled “Workflow Maturity”

The workflow is very mature with minimal issues. Two considerations:

1. GLM Pro Subscription 5-Hour Quota

Section titled “1. GLM Pro Subscription 5-Hour Quota”

Parallel Claude Code shells quickly hit GLM API quota limits during batch production, causing content generation interruption.

Solutions:

  • Implement rate limiting or sequential queuing
  • Monitor quota usage to avoid sudden interruption

2. Tedious Manual Review

Section titled “2. Tedious Manual Review”

Manual review of each article takes a few minutes, but with large article counts, it becomes tedious. This is the only remaining manual step and the efficiency bottleneck.

Optimization Directions

Section titled “Optimization Directions”

Future considerations:

  • AI automatic review mechanism
  • Quality scoring system
  • Auto-publish to official WeChat account

3. AI Autonomous Life Planning

Section titled “3. AI Autonomous Life Planning”

A popular article on X about a foreign female executive using Claude Code for AI autonomous work and life planning.

Reading through it, the main capability is Claude Code’s engineering and ecosystem. I can do this too — and much of my current daily work like rapid app development, batch WeChat article publishing — has already been automated via Claude Code.

My Practice

Section titled “My Practice”

Compared to the article’s case:

AreaArticle CaseMy Practice
Software DevelopmentHuman + AI AssistAI autonomous, only provide ideas
Content CreationManual WritingBatch AI generation, 50+/week
PublishingManual OperationAI auto-publish to draft box

Next Step: Package these capabilities as software products for others to use.

4. “Am I Dead?” App Viral Trend

Section titled “4. “Am I Dead?” App Viral Trend”

The “Am I Dead?” (Should I Die?) type apps are viral. Assessment:

Technical Difficulty: Low

Section titled “Technical Difficulty: Low”

Using Vibe Coding, let AI autonomously develop apps:

  • 1 week PoC delivery
  • 2 week MVP App Store launch

Risk Points: User Sensitive Information

Section titled “Risk Points: User Sensitive Information”

Needs careful handling:

  1. Privacy Protection: How to store and transmit user sensitive data
  2. Content Moderation: Responsibility boundaries for AI-generated content
  3. Mental Health: Avoid negative impact on users

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

Technically no difficulty at all. Risks are mainly in user sensitive data acquisition and usage. If developing, need more consideration on product design and legal compliance.

5. AI Autonomous Software Development: Thoughts

Section titled “5. AI Autonomous Software Development: Thoughts”

Humans vs AI: No Contest

Section titled “Humans vs AI: No Contest”

Currently, humans comparing to AI in software development execution speed, quality, and efficiency — completely no advantage.

Humans may still have advantages in:

  • How to propose valuable ideas
  • How to validate ideas have value
  • How to make complex strategic decisions

Future of Software Development

Section titled “Future of Software Development”

Software is just an idea. We can propose ideas and let AI autonomously help implement them.

Compared to the internet filled with anxiety, noise, and meaningless arguments, we should calm down and think about the future of software development.

Efficiency Bottlenecks

Section titled “Efficiency Bottlenecks”

The biggest factors affecting AI autonomous software development efficiency:

  1. Human Involvement — As soon as humans participate, execution efficiency drops sharply
  2. Tokens/Cost Limits — Also interrupt AI autonomous software development

Future Directions

Section titled “Future Directions”

2026 Outlook:

DimensionDirection
BreadthApp/Mini Program/Web + API services, plus games and embedded software
DepthUse OpenCode, GLM 4.7 and other alternative tools
ConcurrencyLet AI parallelly, asynchronously, autonomously advance multiple software projects
IntensityReduce investment and participation in AI technology, maintain appropriate distance from AI tech

6. Weekly Summary

Section titled “6. Weekly Summary”

Key Numbers

Section titled “Key Numbers”
MetricValue
App Store Published1 (MoneyMind)
App Store Submitted1 (HeartPetBond)
Batch Articles Generated50+
Human Participation Time< 20 hours

Core Insights

Section titled “Core Insights”
  1. AI Autonomous Development Mature — 1 week PoC, 2 weeks App Store launch
  2. Significant Cost Reduction — GLM 4.7 alternative at 1/7 cost
  3. Humans Are Efficiency Bottleneck — Reducing human participation is key to improving efficiency
  4. Ideas Over Execution — Software is just an idea, let AI autonomously implement

Life is compounding. Let AI develop autonomously, let ideas land quickly.

Tags:
  • AI Autonomous Development
  • App Store
  • Vibe Coding
  • Claude Code
  • BMAD
  • Weekly Review

HeartPetBond App: From Adopting a Cat to AI-Driven App Development Submitted to App Store

Jan 10, 2026
Vibe Sparking

TL;DR: Using the Chinese LLM GLM 4.7 with Claude Code + BMAD V6 + Flutter MCP, fully autonomous AI development from idea to App Store submission in just 12 days. The project contains 62 Dart files with 15,190 lines of code.

Part 1: The Beginning — A British Shorthair Blue Cat

Section titled “Part 1: The Beginning — A British Shorthair Blue Cat”

Recently, my family and I adopted a British Shorthair blue cat. Every day is filled with feeding, watering, playing, and cleaning the litter box — we’re having a blast!

While caring for this fluffy little companion, an idea came to me: What if there was an app to record family members’ interactions with the cat — feeding, exercise, playtime, and more? This would not only help track the cat’s health but also let the whole family participate in pet care together, strengthening our emotional bonds.

Part 2: Technology Stack — Standing on the Shoulders of Giants

Section titled “Part 2: Technology Stack — Standing on the Shoulders of Giants”

Why GLM 4.7?

Section titled “Why GLM 4.7?”

In the Vibe Coding (AI autonomous development) space, the technology has become quite mature. Previously, I used Claude Code + Opus 4.5 + BMAD V6 + Flutter MCP to let AI autonomously develop a MoneyMind App. The entire development process took only 8 hours of interaction with AI, and it has been successfully approved and published on the App Store.

Recently, Chinese AI company Zhipu released their latest GLM 4.7 large language model:

  • Context Window: 200K Tokens (same as Claude Code’s model)
  • Maximum Output: 64K Tokens
  • Price Advantage: Much cheaper than Opus 4.5
  • Performance: According to community reviews, slightly behind Opus 4.5 but with excellent cost-effectiveness

Since Vibe Coding for autonomous app development is now mature, cost-effective, fast, and requires almost no human involvement — with AI generating UI and completing automated test verification throughout — I decided to run another experiment: Can Zhipu’s GLM 4.7 model (from the recently Hong Kong-listed company) also quickly develop an app and submit it to the App Store?

The Golden Combination

Section titled “The Golden Combination”

Thanks to this powerful tech stack:

ComponentDescription
Claude CodeBest-in-class AI Agent system with powerful toolchain and ecosystem
GLM 4.7Chinese AI excellence, highly cost-effective LLM (Opus 4.5 alternative)
BMAD V6Most powerful AI-driven agile development workflow + full SDLC management
Flutter MCPBest MCP support for cross-platform development framework

Part 3: Development Process — Fully Autonomous AI Development

Section titled “Part 3: Development Process — Fully Autonomous AI Development”

Having already successfully developed the MoneyMind App, this AI development process was even smoother.

Step 1: Environment Setup

Section titled “Step 1: Environment Setup”

First, configure the development environment:

  1. Claude Code: AI Agent runtime environment
  2. GLM 4.7: Connected via Zhipu API
  3. BMAD V6: Agile development workflow framework
  4. Flutter MCP: Flutter development toolchain

Step 2: Initialize Project

Section titled “Step 2: Initialize Project”

BMAD automatically scans project status and generates an analysis report, confirming this as a new Greenfield project.

Step 3: Create Product Brief

Section titled “Step 3: Create Product Brief”

Based on my simple idea, AI automatically generated a complete product definition:

  • Product Name: HeartPetBond
  • Target Users: Pet-owning families/couples
  • Tech Stack: Flutter + GetX + Drift
  • Core Philosophy: 1-second recording, frictionless tracking

Step 4: Enter Quick Flow Dev Mode

Section titled “Step 4: Enter Quick Flow Dev Mode”

Selected Quick Flow Dev mode — BMAD’s rapid development flow that doesn’t require generating and maintaining verbose documentation, letting AI focus on code generation and testing.

Step 5: Fully Autonomous Development

Section titled “Step 5: Fully Autonomous Development”

Enter the magic command:

If Claude Code execution interrupts, just enter this command again to resume. AI autonomously completes:

  • 📝 Requirements analysis and user story breakdown
  • 🎨 UI design and Widget tree construction
  • 💻 Business logic implementation
  • 🧪 Integration test generation and verification
  • 🔧 Bug fixes and code optimization

Part 4: Project Results

Section titled “Part 4: Project Results”

Timeline

Section titled “Timeline”
  • December 29, 2024: HeartPetBond idea conceived
  • January 10, 2026: Submitted to App Store, awaiting review

Code Metrics

Section titled “Code Metrics”
MetricValue
Dart Files62
Total Lines of Code15,190
AI-Generated Code11,131 lines
Main Pages~10

Core Feature Pages

Section titled “Core Feature Pages”

Part 5: Summary and Reflections

Section titled “Part 5: Summary and Reflections”

This experiment largely achieved its goals: Even with the budget-friendly GLM 4.7 model, AI can autonomously develop a complete app. I only provided the initial idea, and the rest — design, coding, testing — was all handled by AI with minimal human involvement.

Key Findings

Section titled “Key Findings”
  1. Chinese Models Are Viable: While GLM 4.7 is slightly behind Opus 4.5, it’s fully capable of app development tasks within the BMAD agile workflow
  2. Significantly Lower Costs: Compared to Opus 4.5, GLM 4.7 costs only about 1/7th
  3. Remarkable Efficiency: From idea to App Store submission in just 12 days
  4. Quality Assured: 15,190 lines of code, all integration tests passing

The Future of Vibe Coding

Section titled “The Future of Vibe Coding”

The era of AI autonomous app development has arrived. With a good idea and mature AI toolchains (Claude Code + BMAD + LLMs), anyone can quickly turn ideas into reality.

Life is compounding. Build your pet bond with AI today!

Related Reading:

  • Hands-on Review: Building HeartPetBond App with Claude Code + BMAD + GLM-4.7
  • MoneyMind App Development Journey
Tags:
  • HeartPetBond
  • Claude Code
  • GLM-4.7
  • BMAD
  • Flutter
  • AI Development
  • App Store
  • Vibe Coding

MoneyMind App Approved: AI-Autonomous Development Passes Apple Store Review

Jan 7, 2026
Vibe Sparking

The journey of MoneyMind (省钱思维) from concept to App Store approval—a testament to AI-autonomous software development powered by Claude Code, BMAD V6, and Flutter MCP.

Ready for Distribution: A Small Victory

Section titled “Ready for Distribution: A Small Victory”

This morning, I logged into App Store Connect and noticed something special: the App status had changed from the yellow “Waiting for Review” dot to a green “Ready for Submit” indicator.

A moment of excitement—Claude Code + BMAD V6 + Flutter MCP had enabled AI to autonomously develop the MoneyMind savings mindset app, which had now passed Apple’s official review and was ready for release.

The Review Journey: 14 Days, 4 Submissions

Section titled “The Review Journey: 14 Days, 4 Submissions”

The journey began in early November, with sporadic development continuing through early December. Finally, just before Christmas Eve, the app was submitted for App Store review.

Challenges Faced

Section titled “Challenges Faced”

As a first-time App submission, the review process was particularly rigorous:

  • Stricter review for首次提交 - First-time app submissions undergo more intensive scrutiny
  • Holiday delays - Christmas, year-end, and New Year holidays extended the typical review cycle
  • Screen compatibility issues - Fully AI-autonomous testing missed some screen size compatibility issues, resulting in text overlap and missing features discovered during review
  • Account deletion requirement - App Store required adding account deletion functionality

The Numbers

Section titled “The Numbers”
  • December 23: Initial submission
  • 4 submissions to App Store
  • 3 rejections before final approval
  • 14 days from first submission to approval
  • 1 mistaken version submission (human error in the process)

After 14 days and multiple iterations, MoneyMind finally passed Apple’s official review on its first attempt.

About MoneyMind App

Section titled “About MoneyMind App”

MoneyMind (省钱思维) is built on a simple philosophy:

Make saving money as engaging as gaming, transforming tedious bookkeeping into goal-oriented, achievement-driven daily actions.

Core Features

Section titled “Core Features”
  • Savings Goals - Set and track financial targets
  • Daily Savings - Record small daily deposits
  • Streak Tracking - Build consistency through daily check-ins
  • Badges & Achievements - Gamified progress rewards

For detailed development journey, refer to the original MoneyMind development blog.

How to Use MoneyMind

Section titled “How to Use MoneyMind”

With approval granted, I wanted to celebrate—but first, a real test. I downloaded and installed the app, started a savings plan mode, and set a goal: save an extra 1,000 yuan for investment by January 31st.

Setting a Goal

Section titled “Setting a Goal”

Tracking Progress

Section titled “Tracking Progress”

No lunch today, no Starbucks, 10,000 steps walked—every small action counts.

Home Screen

Section titled “Home Screen”

Analytics Dashboard

Section titled “Analytics Dashboard”

The Motivation: AI-Autonomous Development

Section titled “The Motivation: AI-Autonomous Development”

Vibe Coding Context

Section titled “Vibe Coding Context”

Vibe Coding makes AI-assisted app development accessible—beginners and any AI agent can work together effectively.

The MoneyMind Challenge

Section titled “The MoneyMind Challenge”

The goal was ambitious:

Enable AI to autonomously develop software—including generating code and test code, automatically running UI automated integration tests to verify app functionality. Humans only provide the initial idea, then leave everything to AI with minimal or no human intervention throughout the process.

The Winning Combination

Section titled “The Winning Combination”

This achievement was made possible by:

  • Claude Code - Best-in-class AI Agent system for engineering and ecosystem
  • Opus 4.5 - Best coding language model
  • BMAD V6 - Strongest AI-driven agile development process + software development workflow
  • Flutter MCP - Best development language and framework MCP

Goal Achieved

Section titled “Goal Achieved”

基本上实现了既定目标：让 AI 自主开发 App，我只在最初开始提出最初的想法，剩下就交给 AI 生成和验证 App，在整个过程无需人工参与或者尽量少人工参与。

What’s Next

Section titled “What’s Next”

MoneyMind App Evolution

Section titled “MoneyMind App Evolution”

MoneyMind will continue with AI-autonomous development of new features:

  • Data export functionality
  • Internationalization (i18n)
  • Social sharing capabilities
  • Data synchronization
  • macOS, Windows, Linux, and Android versions

Expanding AI Autonomy

Section titled “Expanding AI Autonomy”

The vision extends beyond MoneyMind:

Breadth: AI autonomously completes development, testing, verification, and deployment for apps, WeChat mini-programs, API services, web platforms, and games.

Depth: Beyond Claude Code, Opus, and BMAD v6—explore alternative tools like GLM 4.7 and other software development processes for AI-autonomous completion.

Intensity: Further reduce human involvement—begin “AI desensitization,” maintain appropriate distance from AI systems.

Concurrency: Enable AI to simultaneously, asynchronously, and autonomously advance multiple software projects.

The Ultimate Vision

Section titled “The Ultimate Vision”

最终实现 AI 在无需人工监督、管理、干预的情况下，AI 自主并行推进软件开发、测试、验证、部署和维护，完成软件的全生命周期管理闭环和自治演进。

Wrap-Up: Key Learnings

Section titled “Wrap-Up: Key Learnings”

The Biggest Bottleneck

Section titled “The Biggest Bottleneck”

影响软件开发效率最大因素是人工参与，比如 App Store 人工送审和人工审核，其次是 Tokens 或费用限制。

The Future is Accessible

Section titled “The Future is Accessible”

Vibe Coding AI-driven software development is technically mature and reliable. The market prospects are vast—this is not a zero-sum game. Both professional software engineers and everyday users can benefit.

Conclusion

Section titled “Conclusion”

MoneyMind’s journey from concept to App Store approval demonstrates that AI-autonomous software development is not just possible—it’s here. With the right combination of tools (Claude Code + BMAD V6 + Flutter MCP) and a clear vision, AI can handle the complete software development lifecycle with minimal human intervention.

The future of software development is autonomous, collaborative, and accessible to everyone.

Authors: Vibe Sparking Date: January 7, 2026 Tags: #MoneyMind #AI #AppStore #ClaudeCode #BMAD #Flutter

Tags:
  • MoneyMind
  • AI Autonomous Development
  • App Store
  • Claude Code
  • BMAD
  • Flutter
  • Vibe Coding

Practical Review: Building HeartPetBond App with Claude Code + BMAD + GLM-4.7

Dec 31, 2025
Vibe Sparking

Core Insight: Can Zhipu GLM-4.7 replace the expensive Opus 4.5 in the Claude Code environment? How does its 200K context window perform within the document-driven BMAD workflow? This post reveals the answers through the real-world development of the HeartPetBond app.

1. Motivation: Why This Review?

Section titled “1. Motivation: Why This Review?”

In the AI-assisted programming realm, Claude Opus 4.5 has been the undisputed king, but its high cost (approx. $15/1M input, $75/1M output) deters high-frequency usage. DeepSeek V3, while affordable, occasionally hits bottlenecks with ultra-long contexts (>128K tokens).

I aimed to verify Zhipu GLM-4.7, a strong challenger to DeepSeek V3, by developing the HeartPetBond app to test:

  • Long Context Capability: Can the nominal 200K Tokens Context support an entire project’s documentation and codebase?
  • Output Length: Can the 128K Tokens Output generate complex Flutter pages in one go?
  • Stability & Hallucination: Does logic crumble during continuous development over several days?
  • Cost-Effectiveness: How much money can it save independent developers compared to Opus 4.5?

Context: When using DeepSeek V3 with Claude Code + BMAD, I encountered errors like API Error: 400 ... maximum context length is 131072 tokens... when the context exceeded 128K. GLM-4.7’s 200K context might be the solution.

With over 10 years of Flutter experience and previous success generating the MoneyMind App using Claude Code + BMAD, I set out to see if this new model could handle the challenge.

2. Setup: Integrating GLM-4.7 into Claude Code

Section titled “2. Setup: Integrating GLM-4.7 into Claude Code”

Switching Claude Code’s “brain” is straightforward:

1. Install BMAD

Section titled “1. Install BMAD”

First, we introduce the BMAD (Building Multi-Agent Development) system, the core methodology that makes AI “understand engineering standards.”

2. Configure ZHIPU GLM-4.7

Section titled “2. Configure ZHIPU GLM-4.7”

Claude Code supports connecting external models via MCP or API adapters.

  1. Register and subscribe to the Zhipu BIGMODEL platform to get an API Key.
  2. Configure the default model in Claude Code (or related MCP settings) to point to GLM-4.7.

3. From Idea to Product: The Automated Workflow

Section titled “3. From Idea to Product: The Automated Workflow”

Following the BMAD workflow, HeartPetBond’s birth followed standard Document Driven Development. Here are the key milestones:

1. Initialize Workflow

Section titled “1. Initialize Workflow”

BMAD scanned the project state and provided a precise analysis:

2. Create Product Brief

Section titled “2. Create Product Brief”

After confirming brainstorming results, the AI generated the product definition:

It even locked in the tech stack: Flutter for cross-platform UI, GetX for lightweight state management, and Drift for local persistence.

4. PoC Verification & Performance

Section titled “4. PoC Verification & Performance”

After switching to GLM-4.7, I was most surprised by its “throughput speed” and “depth of understanding.”

1. Development Speed

Section titled “1. Development Speed”

In just 2-3 days, GLM-4.7 helped me complete 5 core pages for HeartPetBond (PoC phase):

  • Welcome Page: User onboarding.
  • Ready State: State checks.
  • Home Page: Core recording flow.
  • Settings: Complex configuration management.

The code pass rate was extremely high, rarely missing brackets or breaking logic even in deep Flutter Widget trees.

2. Token Usage

Section titled “2. Token Usage”

The cost breakdown is impressive:

  • PoC Phase Total: Approx. 300 Million (300M) tokens.
  • Estimated MVP: For 15 pages and App Store submission, estimated 3 Billion (3B) tokens.

Despite this massive volume, GLM-4.7’s cost is only about 1/7th of Opus 4.5. A Pro subscription (~¥200/month) essentially covers the MVP development needs.

5. Wrap Up

Section titled “5. Wrap Up”

The verdict from this field test is clear: Building mobile apps with GLM-4.7 + Claude Code + BMAD is entirely feasible and highly cost-effective.

✅ Pros

Section titled “✅ Pros”
  1. High Cost-Performance: GLM-4.7 is a perfect “affordable alternative” to Opus 4.5. It significantly outperforms DeepSeek V3 in daily coding tasks while remaining wallet-friendly.
  2. Long Window Stability: The 200K Context Window is real. It maintained clarity without “catastrophic forgetting” even after loading mass amounts of BMAD documentation.
  3. Engineering Ready: Works seamlessly with Claude Code’s toolchain to handle complex refactoring and debugging.

⚠️ Cons

Section titled “⚠️ Cons”
  1. Reasoning Minor Flaws: In very complex business flow designs, GLM-4.7’s reasoning chain occasionally misses a step compared to the meticulous Opus 4.5. This is manageable via human Code Review and clearer prompts.
  2. Multimodal Gaps: Current API support for multimodal inputs (images/files) is limited, requiring an additional zai-mcp-server configuration.

🔮 Final Verdict

Section titled “🔮 Final Verdict”

If you are an independent developer looking for a smart AI pair programmer that won’t break the bank, Claude Code + GLM-4.7 + BMAD is the combination to beat right now.

Life is compounding. Start building your bond today.

Tags:
  • Claude Code
  • GLM-4.7
  • BMAD
  • Flutter
  • AI Development

BMAD Method: How to Turn LLM System Prompts into a Reproducible AI Dev Team

Dec 27, 2025
Vibe Sparking

BMAD Method is an open-source AI-driven agile development methodology and toolkit based on the BMAD Core framework. It includes 19 professional AI Agents and 50+ Workflows covering the complete lifecycle from requirements analysis, planning, and architecture design to development and testing. It has garnered 23.6k stars on GitHub (as of December 2025). It packages system prompts, role definitions, and process instructions into structured assets, allowing you to orchestrate a team of AI experts working together like a real software development team, rather than copying and pasting fragmented prompts.

Why Do You Need a Systematic System Prompt Repository?

Section titled “Why Do You Need a Systematic System Prompt Repository?”

Most AI learners and developers encounter these pain points when using large language models:

  • Disorganized system prompts: You write a good system prompt in Claude today, modify it in ChatGPT tomorrow, and end up with prompts scattered across conversations and documents, unable to find the best version.
  • Unstable AI roles: Sometimes you want it to be a product manager, sometimes an architect, sometimes QA, but you have to re-explain each time with inconsistent results and unstable output quality.
  • Lack of closed-loop from requirements to code: Even if you’ve written many prompts, they’re mostly one-off: requirements documents have one style, architecture discussions another, development and testing yet another. These prompts lack strict artifact flow relationships, making it hard to form a replicable engineering process.
  • High collaboration costs: When you want to share your collection of prompts with colleagues, they can only copy from a long document or Notion page one by one—completely non-toolified.

If you’ve experienced similar issues, what BMAD Method does can be understood as:

Engineering, versioning, and portable AI system prompts + role division + process steps, transforming them into an AI team framework that can be repeatedly reused across various IDEs and AI tools.

What is BMAD Method Essentially?

Section titled “What is BMAD Method Essentially?”

The positioning of BMAD Method can be summarized in one sentence:

Breakthrough Method for Agile AI-Driven Development — A framework that enables AI to participate in development as an agile team.

Its core has three layers:

  • BMAD Core: A foundational framework designed for human + multi-agent collaboration, containing:

    • Agents (professional role personas) written in Markdown
    • Agent Teams composed of multiple agents
    • Workflows (strategic processes) defined in YAML
    • Templates with LLM instructions (PRD, architecture documents, Story files, etc.)

  • BMAD Method: Built on top of BMAD Core, focusing on software development workflows, breaking down the entire chain from analysis → planning → solution → implementation into multiple stages and artifacts, completed by different agents in relay.

  • BMAD Builder / Creative Intelligence Suite:

    • BMAD Builder: Allows you to design your own professional agents and modules on the same framework (e.g., legal, finance, education fields).
    • Creative Intelligence Suite: Provides creative thinking and brainstorming workflows to assist product innovation and narrative design.

In other words, BMAD Method is not just a simple prompt collection. It structures system prompts by:

  • Roles (who does it)
  • Stages (when to do it)
  • Artifacts (what to produce)
  • Tools (where to use them)

All structured into an engineering methodology and code repository that can be ported to your project.

Core Features

Section titled “Core Features”

1. 19 Professional AI Roles to Build a Virtual Development Team

Section titled “1. 19 Professional AI Roles to Build a Virtual Development Team”

BMAD includes a small virtual company:

  • Analyst
  • Product Manager
  • Architect
  • Scrum Master
  • Product Owner
  • Developer
  • QA Engineer
  • UX Designer, Game Designer, Tech Writer, etc.

Each role has:

  • Clear responsibilities
  • Corresponding commands (such as @analyst, @pm, @dev)
  • Corresponding document types to produce (PRD, architecture specs, Story files, QA reports)

These definitions are written in Markdown + system prompts—you don’t need to reinvent how to make a model play a qualified product manager—just call the existing persona directly.

2. 50+ Workflows Covering the Complete Development Lifecycle

Section titled “2. 50+ Workflows Covering the Complete Development Lifecycle”

BMAD Method breaks down the entire software delivery process into 4 stages:

  1. Analysis: Requirements discovery, market research, solution comparison
  2. Planning: PRD, technical specs, game design documents, etc.
  3. Solutioning: System architecture, data flow, API design, UX sketches
  4. Implementation: Story-driven development, code implementation, testing verification

For each stage, the framework provides multiple workflows defined in YAML form with process and call order. For example:

  • Use Analyst to generate Project Brief
  • Use PM to generate PRD
  • Use Architect to generate Architecture document
  • Use PO for document sharding
  • Use SM to generate Story files
  • Use Developer/QA for implementation and verification

This is essentially an executable system prompt process, not a bunch of scattered prompt examples.

3. Scale-Adaptive Intelligence: Three Intelligence Tracks

Section titled “3. Scale-Adaptive Intelligence: Three Intelligence Tracks”

BMAD provides three intelligence tracks:

  • Quick Flow: Suitable for bug fixes and small requirements, generating lightweight technical specs, ready to code in minutes.
  • BMad Method Standard Track: For new features, new products, or medium-sized platforms, from PRD to architecture to UX.
  • Enterprise Track: For projects with high compliance, governance requirements, providing more complete documentation, quality gates, and processes.

In practice, you can run *workflow-init to let BMAD automatically recommend the appropriate track based on goals and context.

4. Story / Sharding System: Documents as First-Class Engineering Citizens

Section titled “4. Story / Sharding System: Documents as First-Class Engineering Citizens”

BMAD emphasizes document sharding and Story file systems:

  • Break large PRDs into multiple Epic files
  • Generate Story files with complete context from Epics (e.g., 1.1.user-registration.story.md)
  • Story files include:
    • Architecture background
    • Implementation details
    • Acceptance criteria
    • Related constraints

Finally, Developer and QA work around Story files—creating a strict mapping between system prompts + documents + code, facilitating version control, auditing, and rollback.

5. Modular Architecture Based on BMAD Core

Section titled “5. Modular Architecture Based on BMAD Core”

At the framework level, BMAD explicitly divides itself into several modules:

  • Agents: Markdown persona definitions (essentially structured system prompts)
  • Agent Teams: Reusable team combinations
  • Workflows: YAML process definitions
  • Templates: Document templates with embedded LLM instructions
  • Expansion Packs: Extensions for specific fields (creative, business, education, health, etc.)

This means: if you’re working on organizing LLM system prompts, you can directly follow this directory structure and abstraction approach, building your prompt repository on the same architecture.

6. IDE & Toolchain Integration

Section titled “6. IDE & Toolchain Integration”

BMAD is not a framework that only works in a specific web page—it has been verified and integrated in many development tools:

  • Supports Cursor, Windsurf, and other AI IDEs via @agent syntax
  • Compatible with VS Code extensions like Cline, RooCode, Augment, Aider
  • Provides command-line tools based on Node.js 20+ (npx bmad-method)
  • Can run complete workflows in browser Web UI

For developers accustomed to asking AI while writing in IDEs, this is much more comfortable than copying and pasting prompts in browser tabs.

7. Token Savings and Update Safety

Section titled “7. Token Savings and Update Safety”

In large projects, you often encounter contexts that are too long and token limits. BMAD’s approach is:

  • Use the document sharding mechanism to feed each agent only the part of the context it needs
  • Official claims up to ~90% token savings (for large projects)

At the same time, the framework is designed with update safety in mind:

  • Your custom configuration is by default placed in directories that won’t be overwritten
  • When upgrading BMAD versions, your personalized agent and workflow settings are preserved

8. Web Bundles and Multi-Model Support

Section titled “8. Web Bundles and Multi-Model Support”

BMAD provides so-called Web Bundles, allowing you to reuse the same set of agent and process definitions on:

  • ChatGPT
  • Claude Projects
  • Gemini Gems

This is very friendly for students researching different LLM performance differences—you can run the same BMAD workflow on different models and compare output quality horizontally.

Technical Architecture: From System Prompts to Executable Processes

Section titled “Technical Architecture: From System Prompts to Executable Processes”

To more intuitively understand BMAD Method’s technical structure, we can summarize each core component in the following table:

ComponentLogical Location / FormContent CarriedRole in System Prompt Engineering
Agentsbmad-core/agents/ (Markdown)Professional role personas, behavior guidelines, communication stylesBreaking long system prompts into role definitions
Agent Teamsbmad-core/agent-teams/Combination configurations of multiple agentsDescribing what an AI team looks like
Workflowsbmad-core/workflows/ (YAML)Multi-stage processes, dependencies, command callsStringing multiple roles and prompts into complete processes
Templatesbmad-core/templates/ (Markdown)PRD, architecture docs, Story templates + LLM instructionsMaking LLMs output artifacts in unified format
Expansion PacksExtension directories / independent modulesDomain-specific agents / workflowsCopying this methodology to other industries

If you’re organizing your own LLM system prompt repository, it’s well worth referencing BMAD’s layered approach: upgrading prompt text into combinations of roles + processes + templates, rather than isolated strings.

From Installation to Running Your First Workflow

Section titled “From Installation to Running Your First Workflow”

Although BMAD looks heavy, it’s actually quite straightforward to get started.

Installation: Pull the Framework into Your Project with One Command

Section titled “Installation: Pull the Framework into Your Project with One Command”

In any project directory, simply run (Node.js 20+):

After installation, a standardized BMAD structure is generated in the project directory, including agents, workflows, templates, etc.

Initialization: Let AI Help You Choose the Development Mode

Section titled “Initialization: Let AI Help You Choose the Development Mode”

Load any agent in a supported IDE, then execute:

BMAD will recommend based on your project goals:

  • Quick Flow
  • Standard BMAD Method
  • Enterprise track

And provide suggested next steps.

A System Prompt Closed-Loop Example from Requirements to Launch

Section titled “A System Prompt Closed-Loop Example from Requirements to Launch”

Based on the official e-commerce example, BMAD’s complete process is roughly as follows:

  1. Analyst: Project Vision and Market Analysis

    • Command example: @analyst Create project brief for modern e-commerce platform
    • Output: project-brief.md

  2. Product Manager: Generate PRD

    • Functional Requirements (FR)
    • Non-Functional Requirements (NFR)
    • Epic list and user story hierarchy
    • Use Analyst’s Brief as input
    • Output: PRD.md

  3. Architect: System Architecture Design

    • Output: architecture.md, including tech stack, system components, data flows, and API specifications.

  4. Product Owner: Document Sharding

    • Break PRD + architecture docs into focused Epic files, such as:
      • epic-1-user-auth.md
      • epic-2-products.md

  5. Scrum Master: Generate Story Files

    • Generate Story files for a specific Epic, such as:
      • 1.1.user-registration.story.md
    • Story includes context, implementation details, acceptance criteria, etc.

  6. Developer: Code Implementation

    • Implement code around Story files (Controllers, components, database migrations, etc.) and generate test cases.

  7. QA: Quality Verification and Tracking

    • Produce a series of QA documents including test strategy, tracking matrix, quality gate reports.

In this entire process, you can see:

  • Each stage corresponds to one or more agents
  • Each agent is backed by a highly structured system prompt
  • Artifacts (documents & code) form strict tracking chains through filenames and directory structures

This is the concrete embodiment of treating system prompts as engineering assets.

Who is BMAD Suitable For?

Section titled “Who is BMAD Suitable For?”

Combined with official documentation and technical articles, BMAD Method is well-suited for the following users and scenarios:

1. AI Learners Wanting to Systematically Organize LLM System Prompts

Section titled “1. AI Learners Wanting to Systematically Organize LLM System Prompts”

You’ve already accumulated many prompts: writing requirements, reviewing code, generating tests, but now they’re scattered across chat history, documents, and your brain.

BMAD provides you with a template for:

  • How to break down roles
  • How to break down stages
  • How to design artifact formats

You can gradually migrate and upgrade your prompt repository within this structure.

2. Small Teams / Startups: Want AI to Truly Participate in the Delivery Loop

Section titled “2. Small Teams / Startups: Want AI to Truly Participate in the Delivery Loop”

For a team of two or three people, hiring a few AI employees is often more realistic than hiring another person. BMAD can help you:

  • Use Analyst/PM/Architect to help plan your product
  • Use Developer/QA to help quickly implement and verify
  • Through documents and Story files, let team members take over AI-produced work outcomes at any time

Especially in scenarios requiring frequent iteration like SaaS, internal tools, e-commerce platforms, this framework from Spec to Code has significant advantages.

3. Large Companies / Enterprise Teams: Scenarios Requiring Governance, Auditing, and Compliance

Section titled “3. Large Companies / Enterprise Teams: Scenarios Requiring Governance, Auditing, and Compliance”

In more serious enterprise environments, casually writing prompts in chat boxes is hard to pass compliance and auditing. BMAD’s advantages include:

  • All instructions are reflected through files and command lines
  • All documents and code are versioned in Git
  • Workflows and responsibility divisions are traceable

This makes system prompts no longer invisible rules, but auditable, reproducible artifacts.

BMAD vs Other Solutions

Section titled “BMAD vs Other Solutions”

From the perspective of system prompt engineering / prompt repositories, BMAD is not the only solution.

Project / FrameworkCore PositioningPrompt / System Description FormIncludes Complete Development WorkflowBest Scenarios
BMAD MethodAI-driven agile development framework + agent team collaborationAgents (Markdown persona), Workflows (YAML), Templates (document templates)✅ Full chain from requirements to QASoftware and product development, teams wanting to engineer system prompts
PromptSourcePrompt IDE and repository for NLP datasets2000+ prompts for ~170 datasets, supporting template language and visual debugging❌ More biased toward dataset tasksAcademic research, benchmarking, building NLP task prompt sets
BMAD Prompt MCPBMAD-First prompt engineering serverRaw prompt → BMAD structured prompt via MCP interface🔁 Focuses on single-task prompt structuringEngineers wanting to unify prompt specifications in tools
Prompt CommonsPrompt governance and versioning in city governance scenariosVersioned prompt repository concept with governance metadata and authorization info❌ More research and concept levelResearch and government projects needing prompt management from strategy/governance perspective

Simply put:

  • PromptSource is more biased toward development tools for different NLP datasets with different prompts
  • BMAD Prompt MCP is a supplement that makes BMAD method into a prompt engineering service
  • Prompt Commons is more like a future vision and framework for city/enterprise-level prompt governance

And BMAD Method’s uniqueness lies in:

  • It designs system prompts + roles + workflows + documents as a whole
  • Provides complete implementation paths from command line to IDE, from Web UI to Git repository
  • Has formed a large open-source ecosystem and user group on GitHub (23.6k stars, 3.4k forks)

Summary

Section titled “Summary”

If we compress this article into one sentence:

BMAD Method demonstrates a typical path to system prompt engineering: abstract roles with Agents, abstract processes with Workflows, abstract outputs with Templates, and abstract the entire collaboration history with Git.

For those running tech blogs, teaching AI, or doing daily projects, what you can learn from this repository is not just which prompt works better, but:

  • How to design structured personas for different roles
  • How to create strict mapping between prompts, documents, and code
  • How to manage the entire AI collaboration process through file structure and version control

Even if you don’t directly use all of BMAD Method’s features, you can absolutely treat it as a system prompt engineering practice textbook, following its directory structure and workflow approach to build your own LLM system prompt repository.

Reference Links

Section titled “Reference Links”
  • BMAD Method GitHub: https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD
  • BMAD Official Docs: https://bmad-method.org
Tags:
  • AI Development
  • BMAD
  • System Prompts
  • Multi-Agent Collaboration
  • AI Workflows

BMAD Installation & Configuration Guide: Best Practices for Project-Level Setup

Dec 20, 2025
Vibe Sparking

BMAD (Build More, Architect Dreams) is an AI-driven agile development methodology framework that integrates multiple AI Agents, workflows, and modules. It supports 18+ IDEs including Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor. This guide walks you through the complete installation and configuration process for BMAD v6.0.0-alpha.

1 | Why Project-Level Installation?

Section titled “1 | Why Project-Level Installation?”

Key Recommendation: Install BMAD in your project directory, NOT in your user home directory.

Benefits:

  • Isolation: Each project has independent BMAD configuration
  • Version Control: Project-level config can be tracked with Git
  • Team Collaboration: Team members get identical configs when cloning
  • Multi-Project Support: Different projects can use different BMAD versions

Post-installation directory structure:

The .bmad configuration directory contains these submodules:

2 | Prerequisites

Section titled “2 | Prerequisites”

Ensure you have:

  • Node.js (v18+ recommended)
  • Bun or npm/npx
  • Claude Code or another supported IDE

3 | Starting Installation

Section titled “3 | Starting Installation”

Run in your project root directory:

You’ll see the BMAD ASCII logo:

4 | Configuration Options Explained

Section titled “4 | Configuration Options Explained”

4.1 Installation Directory

Section titled “4.1 Installation Directory”

Recommendation: Use your current project directory and confirm with Yes.

4.2 BMad Optimized Builder (BoMB) Module

Section titled “4.2 BMad Optimized Builder (BoMB) Module”
OptionRecommended ValueDescription
Custom agents/workflowsbmad-custom-srcStores custom Agents and workflows
Custom modulesbmad-custom-modules-srcStores custom modules

4.3 Game Development Module

Section titled “4.3 Game Development Module”
OptionPossible ValuesDescription
Project nameCustomYour game project name
Sprint Artifacts directorydocs/sprint-artifactsStores stories, epics, temp context, etc.
Experience levelBeginner / Intermediate / ExpertAffects how Agents explain concepts
Game engineUnity / Unreal / Godot / CustomYour game development framework

4.4 BMad Method Core Configuration

Section titled “4.4 BMad Method Core Configuration”
OptionRecommendedDescription
Project titleYour project nameUsed for Agent context
Technical experienceChoose as appropriateOnly affects explanation style, NOT document content
Playwright MCPYesEnables Test Architect automation capabilities
playwright-utilsAs neededAdvanced features from @seontechnologies/playwright-utils

Note: Technical experience level only affects how Agents communicate with you. Documents always remain concise for LLM efficiency.

4.5 Codex CLI Prompts Location (Critical)

Section titled “4.5 Codex CLI Prompts Location (Critical)”

This is one of the most important choices during installation:

Strongly Recommended: Choose Project-specific

Why Project-Specific?

Section titled “Why Project-Specific?”
MethodProsCons
Project-specificProject isolation, version controllable, team sharingRequires environment variable setup
GlobalSimple configurationOnly serves single project, references fixed .bmad path

Environment Variable Setup

Section titled “Environment Variable Setup”

After choosing Project-specific, add to your ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc:

Then run:

This alias makes Codex CLI automatically use the .codex config in your current working directory.

5 | Understanding Installation Logs

Section titled “5 | Understanding Installation Logs”

During installation, you’ll see module installation progress:

Installed Modules

Section titled “Installed Modules”
ModuleFull NamePurpose
bmbBMad Optimized BuilderAgent and workflow builder
bmgdBMad Game DevelopmentGame development-specific Agents
bmmBMad MethodCore agile methodology
cisCreative Innovation SuiteCreative and innovation tools

Generated Agents

Section titled “Generated Agents”

6 | AgentVibes TTS Configuration (Optional)

Section titled “6 | AgentVibes TTS Configuration (Optional)”

After installation, you’ll be prompted to configure AgentVibes voice synthesis:

If you choose to install, it automatically configures:

  • 34 slash commands
  • TTS scripts
  • 16 background music tracks
  • Personality templates

Using AgentVibes

Section titled “Using AgentVibes”

7 | Post-Installation Directory Structure

Section titled “7 | Post-Installation Directory Structure”

8 | Verifying Installation

Section titled “8 | Verifying Installation”

After installation, verify with:

In Claude Code

Section titled “In Claude Code”

Then try:

View Available Agents

Section titled “View Available Agents”

9 | FAQ

Section titled “9 | FAQ”

Q: How do I update BMAD?

Section titled “Q: How do I update BMAD?”

Q: How do I use BMAD in multiple projects?

Section titled “Q: How do I use BMAD in multiple projects?”

Run the installation command in each project. Project-level configurations don’t interfere with each other.

Q: Codex can’t find prompts?

Section titled “Q: Codex can’t find prompts?”

Ensure you’ve configured the CODEX_HOME environment variable and are running codex from within your project directory.

10 | Summary

Section titled “10 | Summary”
ConfigurationRecommended Choice
Installation locationProject directory (not Home)
Codex installationProject-specific
Technical levelChoose as appropriate, doesn’t affect document quality
Playwright MCPEnable (for automated testing)
AgentVibesInstall as needed

BMAD’s project-level installation allows you to:

  • Use different configurations across projects
  • Version control your AI Agent configurations
  • Share consistent development environments with team members

Start using BMAD and let AI Agents become your agile development partners!

Resources

Section titled “Resources”
  • BMAD GitHub
  • AgentVibes
  • Claude Code Documentation
